notes: Grafana's eBPF auto-instrumentation tool (open-sourced 2023, github.com/grafana/beyla), since donated to OpenTelemetry as OpenTelemetry eBPF Instrumentation (OBI). Grafana Labs names projects after Norse mythology; Beyla is a figure from the Poetic Edda (Lokasenna), a servant of the god Freyr. Say it BAY-luh, stress on the FIRST syllable (first syllable rhymes with 'bay'/'they'). The 'ey' follows the standard English Anglicization of Old Norse names — the Bellows Poetic Edda pronouncing index lists 'Beyl-a' with 'ey as in they.' (Strict Old Norse is closer to [ˈbœylɑ], a front-rounded vowel, but English usage settles on the 'they' vowel.) English readers commonly mis-guess BYE-la (eye sound) or BEE-la, both wrong.
